results.caption:
🐉 Meet DragonForce. Not a hacker. A cartel.

363 confirmed victims. 30+ countries. £300M+ in damage from a single summer campaign.

This isn't opportunistic cybercrime — it's an industrialized operation with branding, affiliate programs, and a 80/20 revenue split that makes it more efficient than most SaaS startups.

The dragon has a logo. The dragon has a business model. The dragon has your data.

Know your adversary. 🔗 Full case study in the comments.

#DragonForce #Ransomware #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence #RaaS
results.hook: A mythic dragon forged from circuit boards and ransomware code triggers primal fear while screaming 'this threat is real' — the perfect scroll-stopper for security professionals.

results.topic: The Binary Signature — Ransom Note Decoded
results.caption:
📄 The note arrives. The clock starts. The leverage begins.

DragonForce doesn't just encrypt your files — it sends a message. A ransom note engineered to maximize psychological pressure on executives, IT teams, and board members simultaneously.

ChaCha8 encryption. Cross-platform. No safe harbor across Windows, Linux, or ESXi.

By the time the note appears, the exfiltration is already done. The encryption is already done. You're not fighting a breach — you're negotiating a hostage situation.

Here's what the note actually contains. 👇

#DragonForce #RansomNote #IncidentResponse #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence
results.hook: A forensic analyst pinned under red emergency light reads a physical ransom note while its digital shadow decodes itself on the wall — the visceral human cost of ransomware made tangible.

results.caption:
🌍 Where did DragonForce come from?

Malaysia? Eastern Europe? A distributed collective with no fixed geography? The answer is: nobody knows for certain.

DragonForce emerged publicly on December 6, 2023 on BreachForums. Within 18 months it had absorbed RansomHub, announced a full cartel structure, and attacked Marks & Spencer, Co-op, and Harrods in the same summer.

Attribution in ransomware-as-a-service is deliberately obfuscated. Affiliates are globally distributed. Infrastructure rotates. The cartel's only confirmed address is a .onion domain.

Unknown origin. Known damage. That's the most dangerous combination in cybersecurity.

#DragonForce #ThreatAttribution #CyberEspionage #RaaS #CyberSecurity
results.hook: A shadowed war room with conflicting attribution flags pinned to a world map forces the viewer to confront the terrifying reality: we still don't know who built DragonForce.

results.caption:
💻 December 6, 2023. 03:00 UTC. BreachForums.

A new post appears. No introduction. No backstory. Just: DragonForce is open for business.

The post outlined an affiliate program, a data leak site, and encryption tooling for Windows, Linux, and ESXi. Within weeks, the first victims appeared.

This is how modern ransomware cartels are born — not with a bang, but with a forum post in the digital underworld that most defenders never see until it's too late.

By the time your threat intel team found it, DragonForce already had affiliates. Already had victims. Already had momentum.

The clock started December 6, 2023. Did your intel? ⏱️

#DragonForce #BreachForums #DarkWeb #ThreatIntelligence #CyberSecurity
results.hook: The exact moment a ransomware cartel announced itself to the world — a single post at 03:00 UTC on December 6, 2023 — visualized as a neon-lit dark web terminal screen in a server bunker.

results.caption:
📢 March 19, 2025. DragonForce didn't just evolve — it restructured.

On this date, DragonForce publicly announced it was transitioning to a full cartel model — absorbing other ransomware groups under one umbrella, offering shared infrastructure, branding, and negotiation services.

April 8, 2025: RansomHub absorbed into the DragonForce cartel.

This is the moment ransomware-as-a-service became ransomware-as-a-cartel. The difference matters. Cartels don't just run attacks — they control territory, manage affiliates, enforce discipline, and expand market share.

Summer 2025: Marks & Spencer. Co-op. Harrods. The cartel's first major branded campaign.

We are no longer dealing with lone wolf attackers. We are dealing with organized, hierarchical criminal enterprises with HR departments and revenue targets.

Are your defenses built for that reality? 🐉

#DragonForce #RansomwareCartel #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence #OrganizedCrime
results.hook: A boardroom of shadowed figures in a brutalist power room announce the DragonForce Cartel — the day ransomware stopped being crime and started being organized crime with a press release.

results.caption:
DragonForce doesn't hack you. Their AFFILIATES do.

The cartel built a franchise. You pay 20% to the brand. You keep 80% of the ransom.

This is how 363 organizations got hit — not by a single group, but by a distributed army of criminals operating under one umbrella.

The RaaS model is the most dangerous business innovation of the decade.

#Ransomware #DragonForce #RaaS #Cybersecurity #ThreatIntelligence
results.hook: The franchise model visualization exposes how ransomware has become a legitimate-looking business structure, shocking security professionals into sharing.

results.caption:
363 organizations. 212.5% surge. A peak of 35 victims in a single month.

DragonForce didn't grow slowly. It exploded.

From BreachForums debut in December 2023 to absorbing RansomHub's entire affiliate network by April 2025 — this cartel scaled like a startup on rocket fuel.

The data doesn't lie. The surge is real. Is your organization on the next list?

results.caption:
DragonForce isn't a regional threat. It's a global cartel.

30+ countries. 52% US. 12% UK. 6% Australia.

Marks & Spencer. Co-op. Harrods. £300M+ in impact from a single summer campaign.

No border offers protection. No sector is exempt. The cartel franchised its operations across the globe in under 18 months.

Know the geography of the threat.

#DragonForce #GlobalCyberThreat #Ransomware #CyberSecurity #ThreatMap
results.hook: A glowing threat map with country-specific victim counts makes the global scale tangible and personal for security professionals on every continent.

results.caption:
Your EDR didn't fail. It was murdered.

DragonForce uses BYOVD — Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver — to load truesight.sys and rentdrv2.sys directly into the Windows kernel.

Once there, it kills every EDR process on the system. Silently. Before encryption begins.

T1068. Privilege Escalation. Execution. Blind spot created. ChaCha8 deployed.

Your most expensive security tool: neutralized in seconds.

#BYOVD #EDRKiller #DragonForce #MITRE #CyberDefense
results.hook: Showing an EDR flatline like a cardiac monitor makes a technical attack technique viscerally understandable to CISOs and practitioners alike.

results.caption:
They didn't need a zero-day.

They called your help desk.

DragonForce partnered with Scattered Spider — the native English-speaking vishing specialists who convinced IT staff to hand over credentials, reset MFA, and open the door.

No CVE. No exploit. Just a voice, a story, and a frightened help desk agent following procedure.

T1566.004. T1078. The most expensive vulnerabilities are human.

#ScatteredSpider #Vishing #SocialEngineering #HumanFirewall #DragonForce
results.hook: Depicting social engineering as a phone call that bypasses millions in security spend forces every CISO to confront the human vulnerability they can't patch.

results.caption:
For 9 days, DragonForce operators moved freely through the network. No alerts. No flags. No one knew.

They used legitimate credentials, living-off-the-land binaries, and BYOVD techniques to blind every EDR sensor before the real attack even began.

By the time anyone noticed — the damage was already done.

This is how modern ransomware cartels operate. Not with explosions. With patience.

#DragonForce #RansomwareAwareness #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence #ZeroTrust
results.hook: Nine days of invisible infiltration visualized as a ghost walking through lit corporate corridors triggers every security professional's deepest fear.

results.caption:
They didn't just encrypt your data.

First, they took it.

6 terabytes. Customer records. Financial data. Intellectual property. Quietly siphoned over days — disguised as normal business traffic — before anyone pulled the trigger on encryption.

This is double extortion. Pay the ransom or we publish everything.

DragonForce's affiliates used this exact playbook across 363 confirmed victims in 30+ countries.

Your backup strategy means nothing if the data is already gone.

#DragonForce #DataExfiltration #Ransomware #IncidentResponse #CyberThreat
results.hook: Six terabytes of corporate data visualized as rivers of light flowing out of a building into darkness creates an instantly shareable gut-punch about data loss scale.

results.caption:
Backups were the plan.

DragonForce's affiliates knew that too.

Before triggering encryption, they systematically hunted and destroyed every backup — local drives, shadow copies, network shares, cloud sync points. All of it.

When the ransom note appeared, there was nothing left to restore from.

This is why air-gapped, immutable backups tested quarterly aren't optional — they're your last line of existence.

Marks & Spencer. Co-op. Harrods. Summer 2025. £300M+ in combined impact.

#DragonForce #BackupSecurity #Ransomware #CyberResilience #IncidentResponse
results.hook: A security professional's hands desperately reaching toward a shattered backup vault — with the word DELETED burned across everything — is visceral shareability about the moment all recovery options vanish.

results.caption:
In minutes, everything changed.

ChaCha8. Cross-platform. Windows. Linux. ESXi.

DragonForce's encryption engine doesn't care what OS you're running. It was designed from the ground up to consume entire enterprise environments simultaneously — virtualisation hosts, file servers, databases, all of it.

35 organisations hit in a single month. December 2025.
Peak DragonForce. Maximum velocity.

The encryption wave doesn't negotiate before it arrives.

212.5% attack surge. This is what it looks like.

#DragonForce #ChaCha8 #Ransomware #ESXi #CyberAttack
results.hook: A digital tsunami of ChaCha8 encryption visually consuming a city of servers in real time is the most dramatic way to show cross-platform ransomware scale to a LinkedIn audience.

results.caption:
The encryption was just the beginning.

DragonForce collaborated with Scattered Spider — master social engineers — for post-attack vishing campaigns. They called executives directly. By name. Referencing internal documents they'd already stolen.

'We have your HR records. Your customer database. Your board communications. Pay, or everyone finds out.'

This is psychological warfare layered on top of technical attack.

Marks & Spencer. Co-op. Harrods. Three household names. One summer. £300M+ impact.

The phone call is part of the weapon.

#DragonForce #ScatteredSpider #Vishing #SocialEngineering #CyberSecurity
results.hook: A CEO receiving a vishing call from Scattered Spider while their company burns on the screens behind them puts a human face on the most psychologically terrifying element of modern ransomware cartels.

results.caption:
On April 8, 2025, DragonForce didn't just grow — it consumed.

RansomHub, one of the most active RaaS operations on the planet, was absorbed into the DragonForce cartel structure almost overnight. Affiliates, infrastructure, victim pipeline — all of it folded in.

This is what consolidation looks like in the criminal economy. Fewer players. More power. Broader reach.

The cartel model is maturing. And that makes it exponentially more dangerous.

#Ransomware #DragonForce #RansomHub #ThreatIntelligence #Cybersecurity

results.caption:
Marks & Spencer. Co-op. Harrods.

Three of Britain's most iconic retail brands. One brutal summer.

DragonForce — operating through Scattered Spider affiliates using vishing attacks — tore through UK retail in 2025, leaving an estimated £300M+ in damages across the sector.

M&S alone saw weeks of disrupted online orders, empty shelves, and a stock price in freefall.

This wasn't a nation-state. This was a cartel with an 80/20 revenue split and a help desk.

Criminal infrastructure is more sophisticated than your defenses. The data is in.

#DragonForce #Ransomware #CyberAttack #UKCybersecurity #RetailCybersecurity

results.topic: Full MITRE Kill Chain — All 10 Steps
results.caption:
10 steps. That's all it took.

Here is the full DragonForce kill chain, mapped to MITRE ATT&CK — from first phone call to full encryption.

01 → Vishing call. Fake IT helpdesk. Credential harvested. [T1566.004]
02 → MFA fatigue or bypass. Access granted. [T1621]
03 → Living-off-the-land. LOLBins. Blend in. [T1218]
04 → BYOVD. truesight.sys + rentdrv2.sys loaded. EDR blinded. [T1068]
05 → Lateral movement. Domain recon. Admin rights escalated. [T1078]
06 → Data exfiltration begins. Double extortion armed. [T1041]
07 → Shadow copies deleted. Backups targeted. [T1490]
08 → ChaCha8 deployed cross-platform. Windows. Linux. ESXi. [T1486]
09 → Ransom note dropped. Timer starts. [T1491]
10 → Leak site activated. Pressure maximized. [T1657]

Every step was preventable. The question is which step would YOUR defenses have caught?

#MITRE #AttackAndCK #DragonForce #KillChain #CyberDefense
results.hook: Security professionals will stop scrolling instantly when they see real MITRE ATT&CK codes mapped to an actual live ransomware attack — this is the operational intelligence post that gets saved and shared.

results.caption:
They have a kill chain. You need a defense chain.

Here are the 5 controls that — if properly implemented — break the DragonForce attack at each critical stage:

🛡️ 01 — Phishing-Resistant MFA (FIDO2/Passkeys)
Stops vishing credential theft at Stage 1. MFA fatigue attacks fail completely.

🛡️ 02 — Kernel-Level Driver Allowlisting
BYOVD requires loading vulnerable drivers. Block unauthorized kernel drivers. truesight.sys never loads.

🛡️ 03 — Privileged Access Workstations + Tiered Admin
Lateral movement requires admin credential reuse. Tiered access breaks the pivot.

🛡️ 04 — Immutable Offsite Backups (3-2-1-1-0 Rule)
Shadow copy deletion becomes irrelevant. Recovery is guaranteed.

🛡️ 05 — Network Segmentation + ESXi Isolation
ChaCha8 cross-platform encryption requires network access. Segmentation contains the blast radius.

One cartel. Five breaks in the chain. Which ones do you have?

#CyberDefense #DragonForce #Ransomware #ZeroTrust #SecurityControls
results.hook: A visually dramatic 'shield wall' of five specific defenses gives security professionals immediately actionable takeaways they will bookmark, share, and present to their boards.

results.topic: The Dragon Is Already Inside The Walls
results.caption:
You've been watching the perimeter.

The dragon came in through the phone.

A vishing call. A tired employee. One approved push notification. That was all it took.

While you were hardening your firewall, DragonForce was walking your hallways — using your admin credentials, your legitimate tools, your own infrastructure against you.

363 organizations learned this lesson the hard way.

The most dangerous moment in cybersecurity isn't when the attacker is outside the walls.

It's when they're already inside — and you don't know yet.

Dwell time. That's the game now.

When did your last assumed breach exercise run?

#DragonForce #Ransomware #ThreatHunting #AssumedBreach #CyberSecurity
results.hook: The chilling reveal that the threat is already inside — not approaching — delivers an emotional gut-punch that makes executives and security leaders tag their teams and demand internal audits immediately.
